title: No logs when container is run under gitlab-runner user
body:
### Issue Description

Hi,

In our company we host gitlab runners on Ubuntu 24.04 VMs. The system also has podman installed. 
The apt package `gitlab-runner` also setup a user `gitlab-runner` which is used to run jobs under.

Now, when jobs run (or when I start a container manually) under that particular user, then I get no log output of any container with

```
podman container logs some_container
```

just nothing is print (and yes, the container usually prints a lot). 

Running the container with "normal" users on that VM works perfectly fine. Only running it with the `gitlab-runner` user has this behavior.

What can be the reason for that? Can I provide any additional information for the analysis?

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Install `gitlab-runner`
2. `sudo su gitlab-runner && cd`
3. run some container which has log output
4. `podman container logs some_container`


### Describe the results you received

Nothing is printed (also no error)

### Describe the results you expected

The log output of the container

### Privileged Or Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

No

### Additional information

_No response_

comments.body: Could there be a containers.conf in the runner with logging set to None?  Can you look to see if Podman is writing anything to the journal on the runner?
